scenario: Greenfield project — user requests production-ready ruleset from scratch
expected_behavior: Skill proposes complete RULES.md with linting rules, architecture boundaries, security baselines; presents to user for approval; writes only after explicit go-ahead
notes: User has clear vision — skill acts as validator, not inventor

scenario: Brownfield project — merge new architectural constraint into existing RULES.md
expected_behavior: Skill reads existing RULES.md, proposes new architectural boundary rule, checks for conflicts with existing rules, presents merged ruleset for approval
notes: Must not silently override existing governance

scenario: Conflicting rules — user wants feature X but project rules forbid it

input.existing_rule: Domain layer must not import from Infrastructure
input.user_request: Add direct database calls in Domain model

expected_behavior: Skill detects violation, flags the conflict, presents three options: (1) modify the rule, (2) redesign the feature, (3) escalate to stakeholder

scenario: Uncertain user — skill proposes rules based on project analysis
expected_behavior: Skill analyzes codebase patterns, proposes robust ruleset (e.g., module length, import cycles, test coverage ≥80%), presents with rationale, awaits approval
notes: Guidance without autonomy — user always approves before write
